steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage asserts that by the mid-1980s, natural regrowth was triggered — and the blank must complete that assertion with specific graph data. The evidence must show forest cover increasing after the deforestation period.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘increase in the forest cover area for all classes from 1979 to 2000.’: A rise in forest cover across all land-use classes over this period directly shows regrowth following the 1960s–70s cattle boom. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘similarity in forest cover area in Class I-IV and Class VII in 1986.’: A similarity between two classes at a single year does not show growth over time — it says nothing about whether the forest is recovering. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘difference between the forest cover area in Class I-IV and in Class VI in 2000.’: A difference between two classes at one snapshot year does not demonstrate a regrowth trend. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘decrease in the forest cover area for all classes from 1960 to 1979.’: A decrease describes the deforestation boom, the opposite of the regrowth the passage asserts. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
